disqualifier_reason: Planned stambyte within 3 years (2026–2027) with explicitly limited maintenance fund. Cost exposure 320,000–600,000 SEK per apartment with no reserve — fee increase of 15–30% or special assessment likely.
summary: Appealing Hornstull location and strong livability, but a confirmed near-term stambyte with an underfunded maintenance reserve is a hard disqualifier regardless of score.

research.area_notes: Hornstull is an established, desirable Södermalm neighbourhood with strong transit access, restaurants, weekend markets, and consistent demand. Broader Södermalm median cited at ~106,000 SEK/m² in some 2026 sources, but provided market context of ~80,000 SEK/m² is used as baseline.

macro_update: Riksbanken held policy rate at 1.75% as of May 2026. Mortgage rates approximately 2.6% variable. Stockholm BRF prices forecast +8.4% in 2026 according to analyst consensus. Macro environment is supportive of property values.
extra_findings: Stambyte cost context (2026): replacing pipes and renovating bathrooms in a 72 m² 3-room apartment costs approximately 320,000–600,000 SEK. BRF Torkeln has a limited maintenance fund, meaning this cost would be financed through new association debt, which passes to owners as fee increases (typically 15–30%) or a special assessment. The low monthly fee (4,050 SEK / 56 SEK per m²) is partially explained by this deferred cost — it will not remain low after the stambyte. The listing price at 76,000 SEK/m² reflects a ~5% discount to the stated market context median; however, adding 4,500–8,300 SEK/m² of stambyte exposure erases any apparent discount and makes the true cost materially above market.
